channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump backs Luna push for House parental proxy voting
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) has been scrambling to break an impasse over the push for proxy voting, after Luna joined with more than 200 Democrats and nine Republicans to use a procedure known as a discharge petition to circumvent leadership and force a vote on the matter.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: In the latest poll, 37 percent of respondents approved of Trump’s handling of the economy and 30 percent approved of his approach to addressing the high cost of living in the country.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate advances blueprint for Trump tax cuts, other top priorities
item.description: The Senate on Thursday advanced its budget resolution, teeing up a weekend vote on a blueprint for President Trump's tax agenda and other top priorities. Senators voted 52-48 to kick off consideration of the measure. A simple majority was needed.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: How to find out if your car is foreign-made
item.description: President Donald Trump recently announced a 25% tariff on all foreign-made vehicles and their parts—including those used in U.S. assembly plants.
item.dc:creator: Jusolyn Flower, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico to dump millions of gallons of sewage into Tijuana River, US continues to be a dumping ground: Official
item.description: Mexico will dump 400 million gallons of sewage into the Tijuana River, which could spill into the united States, a San Diego official said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Businessman who scammed ex-NBA star Dwight Howard in fake bid for WNBA team gets 12 years in prison
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Georgia businessman who scammed former NBA players Dwight Howard and Chandler Parsons out of millions of dollars was sentenced Thursday to more than 12 years in federal prison. A Manhattan jury in October convicted Calvin Darden Jr.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dog saved after falling over 100 feet at Colorado National Monument
item.description: What started as a dog owner's worst nightmare ended in a heartwarming reunion thanks in part to a multi-agency effort by Colorado responders.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Kiser, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon watchdog to review Hegseth’s use of Signal app to convey plans for Houthi strike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon’s acting inspector general announced Thursday that he would review Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s use of the Signal messaging app to convey plans for a military strike against Houthi militants in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: K-12 schools must sign certification against DEI to receive federal money, administration says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As a condition for receiving federal money, the Trump administration is ordering K-12 schools to certify that they are following federal civil rights laws and ending any discriminatory diversity, equity and inclusion practices.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers propose controversial medically-assisted suicide bill
item.description: Minnesota state lawmakers are re-introducing a controversial bill legalizing medically-assisted death, which is currently legal in 10 states and Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's how newly imposed tariffs will affect California products
item.description: President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs could significantly impact California’s exports, such as automobiles, computer equipment and semiconductors, according to a study from the Public Policy Institute of California.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Yale fires legal scholar amid review of possible ties to 'sham charity' for designated terror group
item.description: Yale Law School has fired an Iranian scholar, accusing her of refusing to cooperate as it probed allegations that she is involved with a group that the U.S. calls a “sham charity” for a designated terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What you do before and during a tornado could mean the difference between life and death
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — With new innovations and technology, scientists have learned from major tornado outbreaks over the years how to improve safety guidance. During tornado season, forecasters try to get the word out about staying safe during a storm — but also planning for it ahead of time.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's tariffs aren't strictly reciprocal. Here's how he calculated them
item.description: President Donald Trump promised tariffs that would raise U.S. import taxes high enough to mirror what others assess as trade penalties on American goods.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What Trump's trade war means for American families
item.description: Stocks are down sharply as Wall Street firms recalibrate their economic growth estimates amid President Trump's trade war. Economists predict the new tariffs could lead to increased consumer prices and inflation, potentially costing American households between $3,000 to $5,000 more per year.
item.dc:creator: David Lazarus, KTLA 5

item.title: Dow drops 1,600 as US stocks lead worldwide sell-off after Trump’s tariffs cause a COVID-like shock
item.description: Financial markets around the world are reeling Thursday following President Donald Trump’s latest and most severe set of tariffs, and the U.S. stock market is taking the worst of it so far.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Markets plunge in reaction to Trump 'Liberation Day' tariffs
item.description: Stocks plunged Thursday morning as U.S. trading opened for the first time after President Trump’s announcement of heavy tariffs on nearly every nation exporting products to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams to run as independent in mayoral election
item.description: Adams said he is skipping the Democratic primary, despite still considering himself a Democrat, to "appeal directly to all New Yorkers as an independent candidate in the general election.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: This is what forecasters mean when they talk about a 100-year flood
item.description: Weather forecasters sometimes warn of storms that unleash rains so unusual they are described as 100-year or even 500-year floods. Here’s what to know about how scientists determine how extreme a flood is and how common these extreme events are becoming.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bodega cats make New Yorkers' hearts purr, even if they violate state regulations
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City’s “bodega cats” are beloved fixtures in the Big Apple — but they’re on the wrong side of the law.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO and JULIE WALKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Juice sold from New York to wineries in 12 states recalled over potentially deadly toxin
item.description: A wine juice wholesaler in New York has recalled a pumpkin wine juice sold to wineries in a dozen states because it contains a toxin that could be deadly.
item.dc:creator: Madalyn Buursma, KRON4

item.title: Speaker Johnson scrambles for solutions to parental proxy voting impasse
item.description: Mike Johnson is scrambling to break the impasse over the push to allow proxy voting for new parents, as the standoff between GOP leadership and Anna Paulina Luna hardens.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Storms kill 7 in the South and Midwest as forecasters warn of catastrophic rains, floods this week
item.description: Violent storms and tornadoes have torn through cities from Oklahoma to Indiana during what could be a record period of deadly weather and flooding.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin And Hallie Golden, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: FBI arrests once-deported Las Vegas MS-13 leader accused in several murders
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- The FBI has arrested a high-ranking MS-13 leader based in Las Vegas who is accused of orchestrating 11 murders, federal officials said. Joel Vargas-Escobar faces charges, including racketeering, for several murders, federal officials said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New bill seeks to alleviate burden on Florida citrus growers as orange groves disappear
item.description: As Florida citrus growers continue to face hardships, causing some to make the difficult decision to sell their orange groves, a new piece of bipartisan legislation is aiming to alleviate some of the burden.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Montana's anti-transgender bathroom restrictions are on hold under a judge's order
item.description: HELENA, Mont. (AP) — A judge on Wednesday temporarily blocked a Montana law that restricts transgender people's use of bathrooms in public buildings. The measure, which Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police say the 2023 Nashville school shooter hid mental health issues from doctors and family
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The shooter behind the 2023 Nashville elementary school attack that killed six people, including three children, had been obsessively planning it for years while hiding mental health issues from family and doctors, a police report released Wednesday reveals.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, KIMBERLEE KRUESI and TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's 'Liberation Day' tariffs: Here's how much countries are getting hit
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday announced he was imposing reciprocal tariffs on dozens of nations, citing what he called years of unfair trade practices.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: District attorney lashes out at ICE for detaining suspect in Boston during a trial
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A district attorney in Boston harshly criticized the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement on Wednesday after one of its agents detained a suspect while he was on trial. ICE agent Brian Sullivan took Wilson Martell-Lebron, 49, into custody last week as he was leaving court.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gravedigger arrested after baby's corpse found on West Virginia cemetery's grounds
item.description: A gravedigger at a Cabell County cemetery was arrested on Wednesday after a baby's corpse from 1982 and a busted casket were found at the Baylous Cemetery last week.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Taylor, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Amazon's last-minute bid for TikTok comes as a US ban on the platform is set to take effect Saturday
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Amazon has put in a bid to purchase TikTok, a Trump administration official said Wednesday, in an eleventh-hour pitch as a U.S. ban on the platform is set to go into effect Saturday.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Charges dismissed against Illinois man accused of assault by Rep. Nancy Mace
item.description: Federal prosecutors moved to dismiss charges Tuesday against a man U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of assaulting her during an event at the Capitol last year.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Brams, KRON4

item.title: Trump announces sweeping 'reciprocal' tariffs
item.description: President Donald Trump announced plans for sweeping tariffs Wednesday, saying “our country has been looted, pillaged, raped, plundered” by other nations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: NASA astronaut Sunita Williams reunites with dogs after 9-month stay in space
item.description: NASA astronaut Suni Williams returned to Earth after a nine-month stay on the International Space Station.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, Fox 5

item.title: Final report details investigation into deadly 2023 Covenant School shooting
item.description: The Covenant School shooting report touches on the shooter's planning and the details unveiled during the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, Fox 5

item.title: NFL to use camera technology to measure first downs next season, approves more rule changes
item.description: The NFL announced a multitude of rule changes on Tuesday, including a new way to measure first downs for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bush, Fox 5

item.title: Attorney General instructs prosecutors to seek death penalty for Luigi Mangione
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi instructed prosecutors to seek the death penalty for Luigi Mangione, the man accused of killing UnitedHealthCare's CEO.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5
